insights: Hardware category resolution time median increased by 845.5% from 169.30 hours pre-July 2023 to 1600.80 hours post-July 2023, with a sharp structural break in July 2023 marking the onset of sustained degradation.

The resolution time increase is universal across all locations, priorities (Critical and High), and assignment groups (Service Desk and Network), indicating a systemic operational issue rather than a compositional or workload-distribution problem.

No meaningful change in incident description length, word count, or vocabulary composition occurred between pre- and post-July 2023 periods, ruling out shifts in incident type, severity perception, or technical complexity as explanatory factors.

Australia’s share of Hardware incidents rose from 22% to 60% post-July 2023 while UK’s fell from 22% to 6%, yet all locations experienced ~850–900% median resolution time increases — confirming that geographic mix shift is not driving the performance decline.

Hardware’s resolution time trend shows continuous monthly acceleration (255.77 hours/month slope) after July 2023, while Network category resolution time declined (-3.76 hours/month), highlighting a category-specific operational breakdown rather than enterprise-wide service deterioration.

The extreme right-skewed resolution time distribution (mean 488.7 hours vs median 201.5 hours) suggests persistent long-tail delays in Hardware incident closure, now severely amplified post-July 2023 — increasing risk of SLA breaches, customer dissatisfaction, and reputational exposure.
summary: The core driver of rising resolution times is a severe, systemic degradation in Hardware incident handling that began abruptly in July 2023 — median resolution time surged 845.5% and continued accelerating monthly, affecting every location, priority level, and assignment group uniformly. Compositional explanations (geographic mix, priority distribution, assignment group allocation, or incident complexity reflected in short descriptions) have been rigorously ruled out, confirming the root cause lies outside incident attributes and points to an external operational change — such as staffing reductions, process redesign, tooling changes, or knowledge transfer gaps introduced around that time. This is not a demand-side or complexity-driven issue but a supply-side operational failure specific to Hardware support. Given the magnitude and universality of the impact, immediate investigation into organizational or procedural changes implemented in July 2023 — particularly those affecting Hardware triage, escalation paths, or resource capacity — is the highest-priority next step.
